subject: Advanced Java Programming
description: Marking guidelines for Java coding exercises and assessments.
importantNote: Java exercises are assessed on correctness, code quality, OOP design, and adherence to Java conventions.

general: Correctness: Does the code compile and produce the expected output for all test cases?
Code Quality: Is the code readable, well-structured, and following Java naming conventions?
OOP Design: Are classes, interfaces, and inheritance used appropriately?
Efficiency: Is the algorithm and data structure choice appropriate for the problem?
Error Handling: Are exceptions handled correctly with appropriate types?
Java Idioms: Does the code use Java idioms (streams, Optional, generics) where appropriate?

rules.condition: Code compiles but produces incorrect output for some test cases
rules.marksAwarded: Partial credit based on passing test cases (typically 50-70%)

rules.condition: Correct logic but compilation errors (e.g. missing semicolons, wrong types)
Correct solution but uses raw types instead of generics
Correct but ignores exceptions or swallows errors silently
Does not follow Java naming conventions (e.g. camelCase for methods)
rules.marksAwarded: Deduct up to 10% for convention violations

rules.condition: Missing Javadoc or meaningful comments for complex logic

rules.marksAwarded: Deduct 20-30% for race conditions or missing synchronisation

expectations: Basic Java syntax correct (semicolons, braces, type declarations)
Correct primitive and String types used
Simple control flow (if-else, for, while) works correctly
commonErrors: Missing semicolons at end of statements
Using == to compare String values instead of .equals()
Incorrect variable scope (using variable outside its block)
Integer overflow when using int for large numbers

expectations: OOP design with appropriate use of classes, constructors, and access modifiers
Correct use of Collections Framework (List, Map, Set)
toString(), equals(), hashCode() overridden where appropriate
Code follows SOLID principles at a basic level
commonErrors: Mutable fields exposed without defensive copying
NullPointerException due to missing null checks
Using ArrayList where a Set would prevent duplicates
Catching Exception broadly instead of specific exceptions

expectations: Stream API and lambda expressions used for data processing
CompletableFuture or ExecutorService for concurrent tasks
Design patterns applied correctly where appropriate
Optional used instead of null returns
Clean architecture with separation of concerns
commonErrors: Race conditions in concurrent code without proper synchronisation
Stream reuse (stream can only be consumed once)
Blocking the main thread unnecessarily in async code

criteria.description: All arithmetic operations correct, printf used with %.2f, integer division demonstrated, compiles cleanly
All operations correct but printf formatting minor issues, or integer division missing
Most operations correct, 1-2 errors in calculation or output format
Some operations correct, significant formatting issues or multiple errors
Basic structure present, compiles, but mostly incorrect
Does not compile or completely incorrect
Both if-else and switch expression correct, condition order correct (15 first), clean code
if-else correct, switch expression missing or minor issues
One approach correct but condition order wrong (e.g. checks 3 before 15), some numbers misclassified
Basic loop structure present but logic significantly flawed
Does not compile or no meaningful attempt
All classes/interfaces correct, polymorphism demonstrated, area formulas correct, max algorithm works, clean OOP design
All features work but minor design issues (e.g. missing @Override, fields not final)
Most features work, but interface default method or polymorphism not fully demonstrated
Basic class structure present, formulas correct, but inheritance or interface not used correctly
Attempt at OOP structure, significant design issues
Minimal OOP elements, mostly procedural
Does not compile or no meaningful attempt
All 5 stream queries correct, Collectors.groupingBy() used correctly, Optional handled, method references used
All queries produce correct results, minor style issues (lambdas instead of method references)
4 of 5 queries correct, groupingBy or max() missing or incorrect
Basic filter and map work, but groupingBy or Collectors not used correctly
Streams used but most queries incorrect or missing
Minimal stream usage, mostly loop-based
Does not compile or no meaningful attempt
Thread-safe BankAccount with AtomicLong CAS loop correct, CompletableFuture used properly, allOf() waits correctly, exception handling present
All features work correctly, minor issues (e.g. not using AutoCloseable for pool shutdown)
BankAccount thread-safe, CompletableFuture used but allOf() or exceptionally() missing
Concurrent code present but race conditions possible, or CompletableFuture not used
Some concurrency attempted, significant thread-safety issues
Basic BankAccount without thread-safety, no CompletableFuture
Does not compile or no meaningful attempt
Immutable HttpRequest, fluent Builder with validation, Optional for body, unmodifiable headers map, demonstration of all three cases
Builder pattern correct, immutability mostly enforced, validation present
Builder works but immutability not fully enforced, or validation missing
Builder pattern attempted, fluent API present, but significant design issues
Some Builder structure present, but not following the pattern correctly
Minimal attempt, essentially a constructor not a Builder
Does not compile or no meaningful attempt
